warnings: Codex-agent run; no OpenAI API runner used.
theme_definition: Infrastructure required to deliver, condition, back up, cool, and interconnect electrical power for AI-oriented data centers.

demand_drivers.driver: AI workloads increase data center electricity density and grid interconnection urgency.
demand_drivers.evidence_summary: Public research and reporting describe AI/hyperscale data centers as a growing contributor to electricity demand and local grid stress.

demand_drivers.driver: Hyperscaler and colocation capex flows into electrical distribution, cooling, power conversion, backup power, and grid-interconnection work.
demand_drivers.evidence_summary: Inferred from physical data center architecture and candidate filings.

why_now: Grid interconnection and power availability are becoming limiting factors.
Candidate filings explicitly reference data centers, power, backlog, or related infrastructure demand.
bottleneck_hypotheses: custom switchgear and power control rooms
electrical contracting and utility interconnection labor
major_risks: AI data center capex overbuild or pause
commodity electrical inputs have lower pricing power
summary: The theme is real and structural, but crowded. The best Serenity candidates are likely second-order suppliers where data center demand creates hard capacity or qualification bottlenecks without fully repriced valuations.
